name: Manipur
landmark: Loktak Lake's floating phumdis and Keibul Lamjao National Park
stopoverNote: Fly into Imphal (IMF), the only practical air gateway; Dimapur/Guwahati rail links mean very long road transfers. Indian visitors need a Manipur Inner Line Permit: the official portal issues Temporary ILP for short visits, with 2026 guidance showing a ₹100 fee after approval, plus kiosks at Imphal Airport and major entry gates. As of 2026, Manipur remains security-sensitive after the 2023–2024 ethnic unrest: keep plans flexible, check current government/hotel advisories daily, expect checkpoints or curfews, and treat Imphal Valley and Loktak as the practical core; hill-district trips such as Ukhrul should be attempted only with trusted local guidance and confirmed safe conditions.

diet.vegetarian: Vegetarian eating is manageable in Imphal, Moirang and larger hotels: ask for rice, dal, kangshoi/chamthong vegetable stew, ooti, singju without dried fish, veg momos, aloo dishes and chak-hao black-rice dessert. Traditional eromba usually uses ngari fermented fish, and many chutneys/salads add dried fish, so clarify before ordering.
diet.vegan: Vegan travel is possible but needs clear requests. Manipuri food uses many vegetables and mustard oil, but dairy can appear in tea, sweets and chak-hao kheer, while fish/fermented fish may be hidden in eromba, singju, morok metpa or broths. In Moirang, Loktak homestays and Ukhrul, pre-request rice, kangshoi/chamthong, boiled greens and oil-only cooking without fish, egg, milk, curd, butter, ghee or paneer.
phrases: Meiteilon: Nga-ari yaodana chaak pithok-u = please serve food without fermented fish
Hindi: Maas, machhli, anda mat daliyega = please do not add meat, fish or egg
English/Hindi: Vegan food chahiye; doodh, ghee, paneer, dahi nahi = I need vegan food; no milk, ghee, paneer or curd

links.label: Incredible India — Keibul Lamjao National Park

cities.name: Imphal
cities.notes: The valley capital is the safest logistics base for Kangla Fort, Ima Keithel, WWII sites, temples, museums, food and permit checks.

cities.name: Moirang and Loktak Lake
cities.notes: Moirang is the practical base for Loktak Lake, Sendra views, INA history and Keibul Lamjao's floating wetland habitat.

cities.name: Ukhrul
cities.notes: A highland Tangkhul Naga district known for Shirui lilies, hill walks and Longpi pottery, but it should be treated as conditional while hill-district advisories remain fluid.

itinerary.sim: Jio and Airtel are generally usable in Imphal and around Moirang/Loktak, while BSNL may help in hill pockets. Expect patchy data, shutdowns during security incidents and weak coverage on Ukhrul hill roads; download maps, permits and hotel contacts offline.

Imphal.name: Imphal
Imphal.note: Three days gives one light arrival/permit day, one royal-market cluster day and one WWII/heritage loop. Keep movement daylight-oriented if curfews or checkpoints are active.

days.theme: Arrival, ILP check and Ima Keithel
days.note: Keep the first day central: complete ILP/admin checks, confirm the latest local advisory with your hotel and avoid late-evening road movement.

activities.name: Imphal arrival and Inner Line Permit check
activities.tip: Apply on the official portal before travel or use the airport kiosk; carry government ID, address proof and a passport-style photo. Verify fee/category before payment.

activities.name: Ima Keithel / Khwairamband Bazaar
activities.tip: Go in daylight, ask before photographing vendors and look for handloom sections as well as produce lanes.

activities.name: Local Manipuri dinner around central Imphal
activities.tip: Vegetarians should ask specifically for no ngari/fermented fish in eromba, singju or chutneys.

meals: Chak-hao black-rice kheer or coconut version

days.theme: Kangla Fort, temples and state heritage
days.note: This is a compact central-Imphal culture day. Confirm Kangla closing day/timings locally before setting out, because security or maintenance closures can change access.

activities.name: Kangla Fort and Kangla Museum
activities.tip: Recent public guides still list ₹10 and Wednesday closure, but treat the fee as locally verified-on-arrival rather than guaranteed.

activities.name: Shree Govindajee Temple
activities.tip: Morning aarti is the most atmospheric time; dress modestly.

activities.name: Manipur State Museum
activities.tip: Useful context for tribal textiles, royal history and polo culture; verify opening day locally.

days.theme: WWII memory and valley craft loop
days.note: Keep the route to the Imphal valley and nearby villages; do not extend toward sensitive hill or border areas without fresh local clearance.

activities.name: Imphal War Cemetery
activities.tip: Plan a quiet weekday visit and follow Commonwealth War Graves site etiquette.

activities.name: Red Hill / India Peace Memorial
activities.tip: About 16 km from Imphal on the Tiddim Road; pair it with valley-only stops.

activities.name: Mutua Museum at Andro
activities.tip: Confirm access before going; it is about 26 km from Imphal and gives a compact Northeast craft-and-house-form overview.

activities.name: Andro pottery or handloom shopping stop

days.theme: Safety buffer or light city reset
days.note: Use this optional day if an advisory, curfew or rain delay disrupts the valley itinerary. Do not force remote sightseeing when conditions are unclear.

activities.name: Advisory and transport buffer
activities.tip: Ask your hotel and driver about same-day checkpoints before any outing.

Moirang and Loktak Lake.name: Moirang and Loktak Lake
Moirang and Loktak Lake.note: Two days lets you see Loktak at sunrise/sunset, the INA/Moirang cluster and Keibul Lamjao without making a tiring out-and-back from Imphal.

days.theme: Imphal to Moirang, INA history and Loktak sunset
days.note: Imphal to Moirang/Loktak is about 45–50 km and usually 1.5–2 hours by road; leave after a daylight security check and avoid returning late at night.

activities.name: Drive from Imphal to Moirang / Sendra side
activities.tip: Rates move with fuel, checkpoints and vehicle availability; fix return timing before departure.

activities.name: INA Museum and Moirang INA flag site
activities.tip: Public sources often list low entry fees but no recent official fee sheet; confirm timing at the gate.

activities.name: Sendra viewpoint over Loktak Lake
activities.tip: Late afternoon light is best for the phumdi patterns.

activities.name: Short Loktak boat ride around phumdis
activities.tip: Recent guide ranges vary widely; use life jackets and agree the route, duration and price before boarding.

days.note: Sangai deer sightings are never guaranteed; go early and keep voices low. Roads, park access and boat movements may be affected by weather or security instructions.

activities.name: Keibul Lamjao National Park watchtower visit
activities.tip: Forest Department watch towers overlook the floating grassland habitat; verify entry and guide rules locally.

activities.name: Sangai deer and wetland birdwatching
activities.tip: November–March has better birding odds and cooler weather.

activities.name: Lakeside fisher village / phumdi culture walk
activities.tip: Go with a local host and avoid stepping onto unstable phumdis without permission.

activities.name: Return to Imphal or second Loktak sunset

days.theme: Slow lake morning and Bishnupur stop
days.note: Use this optional day only if you want slower lake time or need a weather/security buffer before returning to Imphal.

Ukhrul.name: Ukhrul
Ukhrul.note: Ukhrul is rewarding in Shirui lily season but conditional in the current security climate; skip it unless your hotel/local operator confirms safe road movement and accommodation before departure.

days.note: Only travel in daylight with a known local driver after checking the day's advisory. The hilly NH 202 drive is roughly 80–85 km and commonly 3–4 hours with stops, but delays can be longer.

activities.name: Daylight drive from Imphal to Ukhrul
activities.tip: Avoid independent night movement and do not proceed through tense checkpoints or protest routes.

activities.name: Ukhrul town orientation and market walk
activities.tip: Ask before photographing people or church/community spaces.

activities.name: Tangkhul food or homestay dinner
activities.tip: Vegetarians/vegans must pre-request meals; meat and fermented fish can be default seasonings.

days.theme: Shirui Hills, lily season and Longpi craft
days.note: April–June is the Shirui lily bloom window and the festival has recently run in May, but road/security conditions matter more than bloom timing. Keep the day flexible and return before dark.

activities.name: Shirui Hills / Shirui Kashong walk
activities.tip: Stay on trails and do not pluck lilies; carry rain protection in May.

activities.name: Shirui Lily Festival venues if dates align
activities.tip: The 2025 official festival ran 20–24 May; check annual dates and security notices before planning around it.

activities.name: Longpi black pottery village excursion
activities.tip: Confirm road conditions and artisan availability locally before adding the detour.

activities.name: Khangkhui limestone cave short visit
activities.tip: Skip if rains or security updates make side roads unreliable.

days.theme: Return to Imphal or safety buffer
days.note: Keep this optional day as a buffer. If the situation changes, return to Imphal early rather than adding hill-district detours.

activities.name: Morning advisory check and early departure

connections.method: Private taxi via Tiddim Road toward Moirang/Sendra
Return via Imphal, then daylight taxi on NH 202 to Ukhrul
Daylight private taxi/shared vehicle via NH 202

extras: Manipur ILP is mandatory for Indian visitors; apply online before departure when possible and keep a digital/offline copy for checkpoints and hotel check-in.

Foreign nationals should check current PAP/registration rules and their embassy's Manipur advisory before planning, because international advisories in 2026 remain stricter than normal India guidance.

Current 2026 safety planning should prioritize Imphal Valley and Loktak Lake; avoid border areas, Moreh and tense buffer zones, and treat Ukhrul/hill districts as conditional rather than guaranteed itinerary content.
Loktak boat, park, museum and Kangla entry prices are not consistently published on official 2026 fee sheets; use the conservative bands above and verify locally before committing.
November–March is best for Loktak birding and comfortable valley travel; May is useful mainly for Shirui lily/festival planning in Ukhrul, with higher rain and security-dependence.
